channel.title: CTV News – Toronto City Hall
channel.description: Toronto City Hall coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: ‘It’s dirty, it’s unsafe’: Toronto mayoral candidate Brad Bradford pitches cleaning up, renaming Sankofa Square
Should Rogers Stadium turn to a sound barrier amid noise complaints? One Toronto councillor thinks so
item.description: As concert noise at Rogers Stadium continues to be the source of frustration among residents around Downsview Park, one councillor wants venue operator Live Nation to find more ways to turn down the volume.

item.title: Drivers speeding well above posted limit has surged nearly 400 per cent since removal of automated cameras
item.description: Speeding in Toronto school and community safety zones has surged since the province ordered the removal of automated speed enforcement cameras, with the number of drivers travelling at least 16 km/h over the limit increasing by nearly 400 per cent, according to a new city report.

item.title: Municipal election could be turning point for non-profits fighting gentrification
item.description: A handful of Toronto community groups buying properties across the city in an effort to keep rents low say the upcoming municipal election could determine whether they’re able to secure thousands more units in the years to come as the ongoing demand for housing shifts focus.

item.title: Ex-Liberal MP Han Dong running for Toronto councillor in Scarborough North
item.description: Former Liberal Toronto MP Han Dong is running to become a Toronto city councillor.

item.title: Report says Billy Bishop airport expansion could impact thousands of homes along waterfront
item.description: A new report from city staff is warning that any expansion of Billy Bishop airport could result in major adverse impacts on housing, congestion, the environment and the waterfront as a whole.

item.title: ‘There is more we can do’: Toronto Mayor Olivia Chow launches re-election bid
item.description: Olivia Chow says she is running for another term as Toronto’s mayor.

item.title: Toronto moving quickly to patch up parts of city ahead of FIFA; expert says it should be the norm
item.description: The City of Toronto has been quietly working to move quickly on a number of infrastructure repairs ahead of the FIFA World Cup, “mindful” of getting the work done ahead of a deadline that will limit construction.

item.title: A ‘friend to many people’: Former Toronto councillor and TTC chair Howard Moscoe dead at 86
item.description: Former Toronto councillor and TTC chair Howard Moscoe has died at the age of 86, Mayor Olivia Chow announced Saturday.

item.title: ‘School lands should be off the table’: Toronto parents rally to block housing development
item.description: Parents and their children rallied outside some Toronto schools Thursday morning against a legal challenge they fear will see schools closed and lands sold for homes.

item.title: Toronto council approves covering half of councillor’s legal fees in integrity commissioner probe
item.description: The Toronto City Council approved on Thursday to cover only half of one councillor’s legal expenses in connection with an investigation by the integrity commissioner.

item.title: Developers complained about demands by Toronto councillor facing OPP probe: sources
item.description: Developers of two Scarborough properties raised concerns about a Toronto councillor asking them to pay a “considerable amount" of money to get their proposals through city processes, sources tell CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Natalie Johnson, longtime CTV News journalist, to run for city council
item.description: A longtime CTV News journalist has joined the race to replace Brad Bradford as the city councillor for Beaches-East York.

item.title: Toronto city councillor reveals he’s being investigated by Ontario Provincial Police
item.description: A Toronto councillor says he’s the subject of an Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) probe and is working to find out more about the nature of the allegations.

item.title: Nominations for municipal elections open across the GTHA. Here’s who is running.
item.description: The race to elect Toronto’s mayor is officially underway. Candidate nominations opened at 8:30 a.m. Friday morning. Those looking to throw their hat in the ring have until Aug. 21 to do so.
